subject: science
question_text: The passage below describes an experiment. several pots of basil growing indoors Franklin grows herbs in his house. He read that lowering soil pH can help plants grow more quickly. Franklin put cuttings of the same herb in six small pots. He watered two of the cuttings with only water, two with water mixed with one tablespoon of vinegar, and the final two with water mixed with two tablespoons of vinegar. After one month, he measured the height of the herbs in each pot. In this experiment, which were part of an experimental group?
options: the herbs watered with only water
the herbs watered with water mixed with one tablespoon of vinegar
correct_answer: the herbs watered with water mixed with one tablespoon of vinegar

subject: science
question_text: The passage below describes an experiment. dumping food scraps into a compost bin Franklin filled eight compost bins with food scraps. He added worms to four of the bins and did not add worms to the other four. He left the bins outside in the same location, so they experienced the same temperature and humidity. After one month, Franklin measured the percentage of food scraps remaining in each bin. Identify the question that the experiment can best answer.
options: Does compost provide more nutrients to plants when food scraps decompose with worms or without worms?
Do food scraps decompose more quickly when mixed with worms or when not mixed with worms?
correct_answer: Do food scraps decompose more quickly when mixed with worms or when not mixed with worms?

subject: science
question_text: People can use the engineering-design process to develop solutions to problems. One step in the process is testing if a potential solution meets the requirements of the design. The passage below describes how the engineering-design process was used to test a solution to a problem. Read the passage. Then answer the question below. studying an architect's design Anthony was a landscape architect who was hired to design a new city park. The city council wanted the park to have space for outdoor concerts and to have at least 20% of the park shaded by trees. Anthony thought the concert area should be at least 150 metres from the road so traffic noise didn't interrupt the music. He developed three possible designs for the park with the concert area in a different location in each design. Then, he tested each design by measuring the distance between the road and the concert area. Which of the following could Anthony's test show?
options: which design would have the greatest distance between the concert area and the road
which design would have the least traffic noise in the concert area
if at least 20% of the park would be shaded by trees in each design
correct_answer: if at least 20% of the park would be shaded by trees in each design

subject: science
question_text: Part of a DNA molecule Deoxyribonucleic acid, or DNA, is a vital component of the cells of every living organism on Earth. This double-stranded molecule composed of nucleotide monomers is passed down from one generation to the next through reproduction, and it contains all of an organism's inherited genetic information. Specific sections of DNA molecules, called genes, contain instructions for making a wide variety of proteins that determine traits and help the body function. The set of rules that define how information encoded in genes is used to create proteins is called the genetic code. Complete the paragraph. ﻿ the set of rules used by cells to create ﻿ from instructions contained within ﻿.
options: The genetic code is . . . proteins . . . genes
The genetic code is . . . genes . . . proteins
Genes are . . . proteins . . . the genetic code
Genes are . . . the genetic code . . . proteins
correct_answer: Genes are . . . the genetic code . . . proteins

subject: science
question_text: A codon is a sequence of three nucleotides in an mRNA molecule that corresponds to a specific amino acid. A codon chart shows which amino acid is specified by each codon. For example, you can use the chart to find the amino acid specified by the codon UGG. First, look for U in the centre of the wheel. From there, work your way outward to find G in the second ring and then G again in the third ring. The chart shows that the codon UGG specifies the amino acid tryptophan, abbreviated Trp. Which amino acid is specified by the codon UGU?

subject: science
question_text: A codon is a sequence of three nucleotides in an mRNA molecule that corresponds to a specific amino acid. A codon chart shows which amino acid is specified by each codon. For example, you can use the chart to find the amino acid specified by the codon AUA. The first two letters indicate the row and column in which the codon will be found. First, use the leftmost column to identify the row that corresponds with the first letter, A. From there, use the top row to find the column that corresponds with the second letter, U. Then, use the rightmost column to find the last letter, A. The chart shows that the codon AUA specifies the amino acid isoleucine, abbreviated

on top of the female part

subject: science
question_text: This passage describes the myotonia congenita trait in goats: Myotonia congenita is a condition that causes temporary muscle stiffness. When goats with myotonia congenita attempt to run from a resting position, their leg muscles often stiffen, causing them to fall over. Because of this behaviour, these goats are referred to as fainting goats. Myotonia congenita is also found in other mammals, including horses, cats, and humans. In a group of goats, some individuals have myotonia congenita and others do not. In this group, the gene for the myotonia congenita trait has two alleles. The allele M is for having myotonia congenita, and the allele m is for not having myotonia congenita. Buck, a goat from this group, has myotonia congenita. Buck has two alleles for having myotonia congenita. Based on this information, what is Buck's genotype for the myotonia congenita gene?

question_text: A population is a group of individuals of the same species that live in the same place. The individuals in a population often have differences in their traits. These differences are called variations. Imagine a population of wild mice in which each mouse has either black fur or brown fur. mice from the population Complete the following statement. In this population, black fur and brown fur are ﻿ of the fur color ﻿.

subject: science
skill_name: Describe the effects of gene mutations on organisms
question_text: The following passage describes the effects of a gene mutation, which is a permanent change in a gene. Read the passage and then follow the instructions below. common carp with large, patchy scales (top), and small, evenly spaced scales Common carp are a type of fish that have scales on their bodies. The pattern of scales on a common carp is established while the fish is developing inside its egg. During this time, a protein called Fgfr1a1 sends signals to the fish's body that direct the shape and spacing of the fish's scales. The signals from the Fgfr1a1 protein result in common carp with small, evenly spaced scales. The Fgfr1a1 protein is encoded by the fgfr1a1 gene. A certain carp had large, patchy scales because of a mutation in the fgfr1a1 gene. Compared to the fgfr1a1 gene without a mutation, the mutated fgfr1a1 gene encoded a form of the Fgfr1a1 protein with a different structure. This different form of the Fgfr1a1 protein could not send signals to the fish's body. Complete the sentence. The mutation in the ﻿ affected the structure and function of the ﻿.

subject: science
skill_name: How can animal behaviours affect reproductive success? Identify evidence to support a claim
question_text: Animals often behave in certain ways that can increase their reproductive success. Read the passage about a specific animal behaviour. Then, follow the instructions below. baboons grooming one another Baboons are found in many parts of Africa, where they live in groups. Female baboons in a group can form social bonds, or close relationships, with other females. Most female baboons form social bonds, but some have stronger bonds than others. Females that have stronger social bonds spend more time grooming, or cleaning, each other. When a female has strong social bonds with other females, more of her offspring reach adulthood than the offspring of females with weak social bonds. This may be because having strong social bonds helps a female handle stress. When female baboons are stressed, the females that have strong social bonds spend more time together. This makes the females less stressed, which can also help their offspring. Why might forming strong social bonds with other females increase the reproductive success of a female baboon? Complete the claim below that answers this question and is best supported by the passage. Forming strong social bonds with other females increases the chances that
options: the female's offspring will live longer
the female's offspring will be around other females
the female will spend more time grooming other baboons
correct_answer: the female will spend more time grooming other baboons

subject: science
skill_name: Evaluate anatomical and molecular evidence of evolution
question_text: Scientists compile many pieces of evidence to infer evolutionary relationships. Here is one such piece of evidence. These pictures show the mouths of three different aquatic vertebrates: a great white shark, a piranha, and a lamprey. Great white shark Piranha Lamprey Which claim is best supported by this evidence?
options: Of the three species, lampreys and great white sharks are most closely related.
Of the three species, great white sharks and piranhas are most closely related.
Of the three species, piranhas and lampreys are most closely related.
correct_answer: Of the three species, piranhas and lampreys are most closely related.

subject: science
skill_name: The carbon cycle: geosphere processes and interactions
question_text: Carbon is one of the most abundant elements in the universe. It is formed in stars and found in a variety of celestial objects, including planets. Carbon is present in all of Earth's spheres and is an essential element for all living things. Amguid Crater in Algeria Scientists think most of Earth's carbon came from carbon-rich interstellar dust, comets or asteroids that collided with Earth billions of years ago, early in its history. Today, so few carbon atoms are exchanged between Earth and space that Earth is considered a closed system. In other words, Earth does not gain or lose a significant amount of carbon from year to year. However, Earth's carbon does not remain in one place. It continually moves between Earth's geosphere, hydrosphere, atmosphere and biosphere, so the amount of carbon in each sphere changes from year to year. Complete the sentences. The total amount of Earth's carbon ﻿ significantly from year to year. The amount of carbon stored in Earth's atmosphere ﻿ each year.
options: does not change . . . does not change
correct_answer: does not change . . . does not change

subject: science
question_text: A population consists of all the individuals of a particular species that live in the same place at the same time. Examples of populations include the wolf population in Germany in 1800, the red mangrove population in the Florida Everglades in 2010, and a population of yeast in a lump of dough today. Population growth is the change in the total number of individuals in a population over some period of time. If more individuals join a population than leave it, then population growth is positive. If more individuals leave a population than join it, then population growth is negative. Individuals join a population by being born or by immigrating from another population. Individuals leave a population by dying or by emigrating to another population. Which equation can be used to calculate population growth?

subject: science
question_text: Ecosystems and their resources are finite, so there is a maximum number of individuals within a population that an ecosystem can support in the long term. This maximum number is the ecosystem's carrying capacity. This graph shows the average number of aphids, small sap-sucking insects, on each leaf of a melon plant over

subject: science
question_text: Read the passage about kelp forest ecosystems. Just as trees grow in forests on land, large brown algae called kelp can grow in dense clusters in the ocean. These kelp forests grow best in cold, nutrient-rich waters. Because of their dependence on light for photosynthesis, kelp forests form in shallow, open ocean. The kelp within kelp forests are considered foundation species by many scientists. Foundation species heavily influence their biological community by making a physical impact on the environment. a kelp forest Inside kelp forests, ocean currents are dramatically slowed by the drag of the algae. The relatively calm environment within a kelp forest provides refuge for many kinds of fish and invertebrates. A variety of aquatic organisms use kelp to shelter their young from predators or rough storms. Even mammals and birds use kelp forests for protection and feeding! An additional benefit of the drag created by the kelp is that it decreases wave action onshore, reducing coastal erosion and protecting coastline habitats from storm damage. It is largely thanks to the physical impact that kelp species have on their environment that kelp forest ecosystems are able to provide food and shelter to such a wide variety of plants and animals. When these foundation species are disturbed, however, there can be cascading effects impacting the entire ecosystem. an urchin barren Among the many organisms that live in kelp forests are primary consumers that feed on the kelp. Sea urchins are particularly voracious consumers of kelp. In an undisturbed kelp forest ecosystem, predators like sea otters, sea stars, and California sheephead fish keep the sea urchin population in check. But when predator populations are diminished, sea urchin grazing increases. When sea urchins graze in large numbers, they can eat away at a kelp forest until there's almost nothing left. This creates a deforested area called an urchin barren. Which location would be the most likely to have a kelp forest?
options: In Biscayne Bay, where the shallow water is warm year-round and nutrients pour in from nearby urban and agricultural areas.
Off the coast of California, where the water that rises to the surface as a result of upwelling is typically cold and rich in nutrients.
At the bottom of the Mariana Trench, the deepest oceanic trench on Earth, where no sunlight can reach.
Near the shore of Lake Superior, the world's largest freshwater lake by surface area and the coldest freshwater lake in the United States.
correct_answer: Near the shore of Lake Superior, the world's largest freshwater lake by surface area and the coldest freshwater lake in the United States.

subject: science
skill_name: Classify matter using physical and chemical properties
question_text: This is a bottle of carbonated soft drink. Soft drinks are made by dissolving carbon dioxide gas and sweeteners in flavoured water under pressure. The result is a bubbly beverage with a consistent taste throughout. When the bottle is opened, carbon dioxide gas escapes from the liquid. Describe and classify a soft drink.
options: A soft drink is composed of only one type of atom and cannot be chemically decomposed, so it is an element.
A soft drink is composed of atoms of two or more different elements in a fixed ratio and can be chemically decomposed, so it is a compound.
A soft drink is composed of two or more substances that can be physically separated and are evenly distributed, so it is a homogeneous mixture.
A soft drink is composed of two or more substances that can be physically separated but are not evenly distributed, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.
correct_answer: A soft drink is composed of two or more substances that can be physically separated but are not evenly distributed, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

subject: science
question_text: This equation represents a chemical reaction. Co (s) + Ni Cl 2 (aq) Co Cl 2 (aq) + Ni (s) Describe and classify the reaction.
options: Two simple substances combine to produce a new compound, so this is a synthesis reaction.
A compound breaks down into two simpler substances, so this is a decomposition reaction.
A hydrocarbon reacts with oxygen to produce carbon dioxide and water, so this is a combustion reaction.
An element replaces a similar element in a compound, so this is a single replacement reaction.
Ions from two reacting ionic compounds exchange places, so this is a double replacement reaction.
correct_answer: Ions from two reacting ionic compounds exchange places, so this is a double replacement reaction.

question_text: A strip of iron (Fe) metal is placed in a test tube with a solution of zinc sulfate (ZnSO4) . Use an activity series to predict whether a single replacement reaction will occur. Complete the sentence. ﻿ metal is less reactive than ﻿ metal, so a single replacement reaction ﻿ occur.

question_text: Which kinematic equation should you use to solve this problem? Cora has an uphill driveway at her house. While playing basketball at the top of the driveway, she dropped the ball. It started to roll down the driveway with a velocity of 0.5m s – 1 and accelerated at a rate of 0.7m s – 2. How far down the driveway did the basketball roll after 4.0 seconds?

question_text: Isabella pushes a curling stone across ice with a force of 30 newtons to the right. The force of friction between the curling stone and the ice is 3 newtons The curling stone weighs 180 newtons. Which free-body diagram represents the forces acting on the curling stone? Submit

subject: science
skill_name: Evaluate claims about natural resource use: fossil fuels
question_text: Every day, humans all around the world use energy. They use energy to drive vehicles, heat and cool buildings, operate appliances and manufacture products. The graph below shows global energy use per year from 1965 to 2015. Energy use is measured using the unit terawatt-hours (TWh). Data sources: Our World in Data and BP Statistical Review of World Energy Select the statement that is supported by the data.
options: Humans used about the same amount of energy each year between 1965 and 2015.
Global energy use increased most years between 1965 and 2015.
correct_answer: Global energy use increased most years between 1965 and 2015.

subject: science
skill_name: Evaluate claims about natural resource use: groundwater in the United States
question_text: Fresh water is a natural resource that humans use every day. Fresh water has many uses, including drinking, cleaning, taking care of livestock, irrigating farms, and generating electricity. Since 1950, the United States Geological Survey (USGS) has tracked the volume of fresh water used in the United States. The graph below shows the volume of fresh water withdrawn, or taken by humans for any use, in a given year. The data were collected every five years, starting in 1950 and ending in 2005. Data source: United States Geological Survey Select the statement that is supported by the data.
options: The volume of fresh water withdrawn per year increased steadily until 1980.
The volume of fresh water withdrawn per year increased every five years between 1950 and 2005.
correct_answer: The volume of fresh water withdrawn per year increased every five years between 1950 and 2005.

options: the layer of gas surrounding Earth's surface
all of Earth's rocks and minerals

subject: science
skill_name: Describe tectonic plate boundaries around the world
question_text: Read the passage and look at the picture. The Sunda Trench is a deep-sea trench that formed as the Indo-Australian Plate collided with the Sunda Plate. Movement at this plate boundary can cause earthquakes and devastating tsunamis. A tsunami is a series of giant waves that may form when oceanic crust is suddenly lifted by an earthquake. In December 2004, an earthquake and the resulting tsunami affected millions of people in countries surrounding the Indian Ocean. Complete the sentence. The Sunda Trench formed at a boundary.

subject: science
question_text: Johannesburg is a city in South Africa. It is located in the Southern Hemisphere. It has an oceanic subtropical highland climate. Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec 5 10 15 20 25 30 35 40 Jan 0 Temperature (°C) Average temperature in Johannesburg, South Africa Jan Feb Mar Apr May Jun Jul Aug Sep Oct Nov Dec 0 20 40 60 80 100 120 140 160 Precipitation (mm) Average precipitation in Johannesburg, South Africa Which statement best describes the climate of Johannesburg? Hint: Summers in the Southern Hemisphere occur in December, January, and February. Winters in the Southern Hemisphere occur in June, July, and August.
options: Summers have higher temperatures and more precipitation than winters.
On average, the summer months are hotter and drier than the winter months.
Winters have lower temperatures and more precipitation than summers.
correct_answer: Winters have lower temperatures and more precipitation than summers.
